architecture: Screens -> hooks (logic) -> services (data); keep screen components presentational.
Navigation via React Navigation; define routes/params in a typed navigator.
Server state via react-query; app state via a light store (zustand/context) — not everything in Redux.
Platform-specific code in .ios.tsx / .android.tsx or via Platform.select.
conventions: Functional components + hooks only; no class components.
Styles via StyleSheet.create — no inline style objects created in render.
Lists use FlatList/SectionList with keyExtractor (and getItemLayout when possible); never map into a ScrollView for long lists.
Handle safe areas (SafeAreaView / react-native-safe-area-context); avoid hardcoded status-bar insets.
TypeScript strict; define a Props type per component; no console.log in production.
lint: Run ESLint (@react-native config) + Prettier; fix warnings in changed scope.
No unused styles/imports; avoid anonymous functions in render for hot paths.
The base ships .eslintrc.js + .prettierrc.js + tsconfig.base.json (via `sr apply-base`); needs devDeps: @react-native/eslint-config, prettier. Fix code to satisfy rules.
Run tests (Jest/React Native Testing Library) for hooks/logic.
templates: scaffold-data: types + api service + react-query hooks (same contract as the web React pack).
scaffold-screen: Screen component + logic hook + registration in the navigator with typed params.
Reuse existing shared components; mirror the app's folder structure.
design-system: Use theme tokens (a theme provider or constants) — never hardcode colors/spacing.
Responsive sizing via useWindowDimensions/Dimensions; avoid fixed pixel layouts.
Reuse shared UI primitives; keep touch targets accessible (min 44pt) with accessibilityLabel.
library-docs: Follow the app's existing library choices; do not add native deps without justifying the linking cost.
